system.description: The first three days of the Drowned Road are a punishing descent through flooded cave passages. The party moves through narrow channeled paths, bioluminescent chambers, and carved Root-Rune corridors. Navigation is challenging; the deeper passages are older and stranger. This montage covers exploration, survival, and the first encounter with evidence that something is organizing the dead.

complications.round1:
The flooded passages require careful navigation. Water levels shift with underground currents. Kaelen reads Root-Runes on the walls as they descend — the older the Runes, the more information she can extract, but they slow progress.

  • Reason: Map the passage network and mark safe routes vs. flooded dead-ends (the passages branch frequently).
  • Agility: Move through chest-deep flood sections without losing footing or equipment.
  • Intuition: Sense which passages lead deeper into the root-network and which loop back upward.
  • Might: Clear a collapsed section blocking the route, or stabilize a crumbling pillar before it falls on Kaelen.
complications.round2:
The bioluminescent grotto: 100ft across, every inch carved with Root-Runes. Kaelen is overwhelmed — she needs time to process what she's reading. Meanwhile, the party hears movement in the eastern passage: a column of draugr, 30-40 strong, marching in organized formation. The party must stay hidden or be detected.

  • Agility: Find concealment positions in the grotto before the draugr column enters (the passage is narrow — they come through one side).
  • Intuition: Assess the draugr column: not wandering, not hunting. Organized. Marching east. Determine purpose and direction without alerting them.
  • Presence: Keep the group calm and silent while the column passes — 30 draugr, organized formation, 4-minute crossing.
  • Reason: Help Kaelen process what she's discovered quickly enough to share before the draugr are close.

outcomes.round1:
Total Success: The party maps three safe routes and reaches the bioluminescent grotto on schedule. Kaelen has time to read additional Root-Runes and discovers: "These markings are older than the Keeper Order. Someone was here before the Keepers."
Partial Success: Progress is made but the party takes one wrong turn, costing four hours and one Recovery's worth of endurance from relentless cold and wet.
Failure: The party gets disoriented in flooded chambers. They emerge into the bioluminescent grotto later than planned — exhausted. Each hero loses 1 Recovery from fatigue.
outcomes.round2:
Total Success: The party conceals successfully. The column passes without detecting them. Kaelen shares: "These are Root-Runes. But this is older. Someone was here before the Keepers." Both revelations land: the dead are organized, and these caves predate Keeper history.
Partial Success: The column passes, but one hero makes noise. Two draugr pause and look back — hold their breath moment. Pass check or one draugr investigates (minor combat: 2 draugr, easily handled but they've been spotted near the column route).
Failure: Draugr detect movement. The column doesn't fully investigate (still organized, not mindless) but a scout breaks off. Combat: 4 Draugr-Thralls + 1 Barrow-Draugr. Column has been alerted to party presence in the caves.
